Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple The Operation Triangulation campaign targets transportation systems using basic social engineering techniques to gain initial access, establish command and control, and exfiltrate sensitive data.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-1003030 [CWE-798]: Unspecified vulnerability in Apple iOS 12.4 allows arbitrary code execution via a crafted iMessage attachment.
stage.description: The attackers leverage social engineering by sending spear-phishing emails with malicious iMessage attachments exploiting zero-click vulnerabilities to employees of transportation systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2018-5002 [CWE-20]: Improper input validation in Microsoft Windows Search service allows remote code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: After successful exploitation, the attackers establish communication with the compromised device using simple C2 infrastructure, such as free web hosting services or compromised websites.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Finally, the attackers exfiltrate sensitive data from transportation systems' servers or databases via simple unauthenticated data transfer methods due to poor security practices.

Scenario.summary: The Operation Triangulation campaign uses basic social engineering techniques to gain initial access to transportation systems, establish command and control, and ultimately exfiltrate sensitive data.
